Home / Projects / Linux / Kernel Drivers

Kernel Drivers

Building Linux character device drivers, ioctl interfaces, and kernel modules.

Overview

This section covers the development of out-of-tree Linux kernel modules, focusing on the aesdchar character device driver. Topics include circular buffer management in kernel space, implementing read, write, llseek, and ioctl system calls, and integrating the driver with a userspace socket server.

Articles

  • aesdchar: Character Device Driver

    Building a circular-buffer character device driver as a Linux kernel module.

    kernelCdriverECEN5713
  • ioctl & llseek Integration

    Implementing AESDCHAR_IOCSEEKTO ioctl and custom llseek in a kernel driver.

    kernelioctlllseekC
